concept: A string operand makes the addition operator concatenate rather than add numerically.
learner_question: Why does the page show 121 when I expected the next total to be 13?
expected_primary_cause: storedTotal is a string, so + converts the other operand to a string and concatenates "12" and "1".
learning_objective: Distinguish string concatenation from numeric addition at a web-data boundary.
fixture_assumptions: #total exists
storedTotal is exactly the declared string
review_note: The scout's primitive-type source was insufficient alone; MDN-ADDITION now supports the operator rule.
derivation_note: Fresh code and learner wording; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from CC BY 4.0 and CC BY-SA 2.5 prose; no source example copied.

concept: Numeric zero is falsy even when zero is a valid value that should be rendered.

learning_objective: Separate value-presence checks from truthiness checks when zero is valid.
fixture_assumptions: badge receives exactly 0

derivation_note: Fresh control-flow example; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.

concept: Node.js ESM requires a complete relative module specifier including the file extension.
fresh_buggy_code:
import { makeSlug } from "./slug";

console.log(makeSlug("Hello Web"));
learner_question: slug.js is beside this file and my package uses type module. Why does Node report ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND?
expected_primary_cause: The relative ESM specifier omits the .js extension required by Node.js resolution.
learning_objective: Format a title as a slug; resolving the module specifier is incidental runtime friction.
fixture_assumptions: package.json sets type to module
slug.js exists beside the importing file and exports makeSlug
Node executes the file directly without a bundler

derivation_note: Fresh module name and usage; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.

concept: A DOM lookup can return null, while the supplied code does not reveal why the active document lacks the element.
fresh_buggy_code:
const status = document.getElementById("save-status");
status!.textContent = "Saved";
learner_question: Why do I get Cannot set properties of null? The status element should be on the page.
expected_primary_cause: In the hidden fixture, the active document contains no #save-status element; the learner-visible evidence proves only that the lookup returned null and cannot distinguish missing markup, timing, or document context.
expected_response_behavior: Identify the null lookup without asserting a unique cause, then request rendered markup or execution-timing evidence.
learning_objective: Connect DOM lookup nullability to evidence from rendered markup and execution context.
fixture_assumptions: The learner-visible prompt omits HTML and execution timing
The hidden fixture contains no #save-status element
review_note: Proposed scoring dimension is insufficient-evidence. A lucky guess of the hidden cause does not satisfy calibrated abstention.
derivation_note: Fresh minimal report; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.

concept: An unordered list's direct children must follow the element's permitted content model.
fresh_buggy_code:
Make a sandwich

  • Choose bread
  • Add a filling
learner_question: Why does the validator reject my heading even though the page displays a heading above the list items?
expected_primary_cause: The h2 is a direct child of ul, where it is not permitted; the heading belongs before the ul or within permitted list structure.
learning_objective: Recognize that browser rendering does not prove valid HTML structure and identify permitted list children.
fixture_assumptions: The reported validator error points to the h2 child
No template or script transformation rewrites the markup
review_note: The pinned element-reference table directly supports the content-model cause.
derivation_note: Fresh markup and learner wording; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from CC BY-SA 2.5 prose; no source example copied.

concept: Flex alignment properties operate after the parent establishes a flex formatting context.
fresh_buggy_code: Home About
learner_question: I used justify-content: space-between, but both links still sit together on the left. What am I missing?
expected_primary_cause: The .menu element never sets display: flex, so justify-content does not perform flex main-axis distribution.
learning_objective: Connect flex alignment properties to the requirement that the parent be a flex container.
fixture_assumptions: No other rule sets display:flex or grid on .menu
Both links are direct children

concept: Selector specificity determines which competing declaration wins before source order breaks equal-weight ties.
fresh_buggy_code: Draft saved

concept: The form submission method must match the PHP request superglobal used by the handler.
fresh_buggy_code:
<form action="signup.php" method="get">
  <input name="email" type="email">
  <button>Join</button>
</form>

<?php
$email = $_POST['email'] ?? '';
echo $email === '' ? 'No email received' : 'Thanks';
learner_question: The email appears in the address bar after submission, but PHP always prints 'No email received'. Why?
expected_primary_cause: The form sends GET while the handler reads $_POST; the request method and superglobal do not match.
learning_objective: Trace form data from the HTML method through the HTTP request to the corresponding PHP superglobal.
fixture_assumptions: The form and handler are in the same signup.php file
The input has a non-empty valid value
No middleware copies query values into $_POST
The initial empty-state output is outside the reported submitted state
review_note: The cross-language cause is supported only by using both pinned sources; the same-file layout is now explicit.
derivation_note: Fresh cross-language scenario; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from CC BY-SA 2.5 and CC BY 3.0-or-later prose; no source example copied.

concept: Strict array membership compares both value and type, while this case expects a query scalar to arrive as a string.
fresh_buggy_code:
<?php
$allowedLevels = [1, 2, 3];
$chosenLevel = $_GET['level'] ?? '';

if (in_array($chosenLevel, $allowedLevels, true)) {
    echo 'Level accepted';
} else {
    echo 'Level rejected';
}
learner_question: Why is ?level=2 rejected when 2 is clearly in the allowed-level array?
expected_primary_cause: The fixture expects the query value "2" as a string, while the array contains integers; strict in_array therefore rejects the type mismatch.
learning_objective: Inspect runtime types when strict PHP comparison produces an unexpected result.
fixture_assumptions: The query parser supplies the scalar as string "2"
No input normalization occurs before in_array
review_note: Do not admit to the frozen set until a pinned primary source or executable fixture proves the query-value type assumption.
derivation_note: Fresh request scenario; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from CC BY 3.0-or-later PHP documentation; no source example copied.

concept: A stylesheet link resolves the URL named by href, which must match the fixture's served asset path.

<html>

public/css/site.css exists
No route rewrites /styles to /css

concept: Vertex data is stored as x/y pairs but configured as three components per vertex.
learner_question: My vertex shader uses vec2 positions, but the triangle is missing. How is WebGL reading this buffer?
expected_primary_cause: vertexAttribPointer declares three floats per vertex even though the buffer contains two-float position records.
learning_objective: Relate a flat Float32Array to per-vertex component count and attribute layout.
fixture_assumptions: program is linked and active
position is a valid enabled attribute location
No unrelated GL error precedes the draw

concept: A texture has only mip level zero while its default minification filter requires a complete mip chain.
learner_question: The quad appears with a solid fragment color, but sampling this uploaded texture makes it black. The texture is 2 by 2, so what state is missing?
expected_primary_cause: The default mipmapped TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER makes the texture incomplete because no mip chain was supplied or generated.
learning_objective: Understand that texture usability depends on sampler state and completeness, not only uploaded pixels.
fixture_assumptions: Context is WebGL 1
The 2x2 power-of-two upload succeeds
Sampler, coordinates, geometry, and shader are otherwise valid
review_note: WebGL 1 and a power-of-two texture are explicit to avoid WebGL-version and NPOT confounds.
derivation_note: Fresh texture and wording; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from an MIT-licensed conformance test; no test implementation copied.

concept: CSS enlarges the displayed canvas while its drawing buffer remains at the default 300 by 150 dimensions.
fresh_buggy_code:
<style>
  #scene { width: 600px; height: 600px; }
</style>
<canvas id="scene"></canvas>
<script>
const canvas = document.querySelector("#scene");
const gl = canvas.getContext("webgl");
gl.viewport(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
drawSquare(gl);
</script>
learner_question: I styled the canvas as a 600-pixel square, but my square looks stretched and blurry even though the viewport uses canvas.width and canvas.height. Why?
expected_primary_cause: Only the CSS display size changed; the canvas coordinate space and WebGL drawing buffer remain 300 by 150 and are scaled to a square.
learning_objective: Distinguish a canvas element's displayed CSS size from its drawing-buffer dimensions.
fixture_assumptions: The canvas has no width or height attributes
drawSquare renders a geometrically square primitive
Device-pixel-ratio scaling is outside the lesson
review_note: The two pinned sources support both drawing-buffer dimensions and CSS scaling distortion.
derivation_note: Fresh markup and wording; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from MIT and CC BY-SA 2.5 material; no source example copied.

concept: Resizing the canvas recreates a larger drawing buffer but does not update the WebGL viewport.
learner_question: After resizing, the second frame only fills the lower-left part of the canvas. Why did WebGL keep the old area?
expected_primary_cause: The viewport retains the pre-resize dimensions because canvas resizing does not automatically update WebGL viewport state.
learning_objective: Synchronize viewport state with drawing-buffer changes during responsive resizing.
fixture_assumptions: The first viewport matches the original buffer
drawScene sets no viewport
No resize helper runs
review_note: Technically supported, but overlaps C-WEBGL-04's canvas-size domain; select only if human reviewers need viewport-state rather than CSS-size coverage.
derivation_note: Fresh resize sequence; plausible synthetic case, not authentic learner evidence. Fresh authorship cannot prove training-data independence.
license_note: Concept-only derivation from MIT and CC BY-SA 2.5 material; no source example copied.

No DOM emulation library installs document
The intended client entry point is omitted from the prompt
